title: Add Stage 2 regression test suite for canonical Formam storage and CLI flows
goal: Protect the Stage 2 Formam baseline with automated regression coverage over canonical deliverable storage, goal-map storage, parent-goal validation, id allocation, and the active Formam CLI command flow.
context: Stage 2 Wave A established canonical Formam storage for deliverables and goal maps.
Stage 2 Wave B wired the active Formam command path through the CLI.
Wave C should close Stage 2 by turning the live implementation into a stable baseline rather than leaving it as a merely functional prototype.
requirements: Add automated tests for deliverable normalization into the canonical schema.
Add automated tests for deliverable markdown round-trip behavior so canonical deliverables can be written and read without loss.
Add automated tests for deliverable id allocation from existing DELIV_### files.
Add automated tests for parent-goal validation against canonical Finis goal files.
Add automated tests for goal-map markdown round-trip behavior.
Add automated tests for the main Formam CLI command flow: form build, form list, form show, and form map.
Verify canonical backlinks from deliverables to exactly one parent goal.
Make the regression suite runnable through the repository's normal test command.
acceptance_criteria: Stage 2 has a real automated regression suite under tests/.
The highest-risk Formam storage and CLI paths are covered by automated tests.
The tests prove the canonical file-backed deliverable and map model behaves deterministically.
The Stage 2 regression suite passes through the project's standard test command.
suggested_commit_message: STAGE2-WAVEC-01: add stage 2 regression tests for canonical formam storage and cli flows

title: Align Stage 2 documentation with canonical Formam storage
goal: Make the operator-facing documentation describe the live Formam system accurately, explicitly naming file-backed deliverables and goal maps as the canonical Stage 2 truth.
context: Formam is now live through canonical file-backed storage under data/form/deliverables/ and data/form/maps/.
The docs must stop presenting Formam as only aspirational or partially planned.
Wave C is the Stage 2 closeout wave, so the docs should clearly reflect the new baseline before later architecture expands beyond this stage.
requirements: Refactor README.md so the project overview reflects live Formam behavior rather than only planned behavior.
Refactor AZQ_BOOTSTRAP.md so bootstrap instructions describe canonical Formam storage correctly.
Refactor AZQ_BUILD_TASKS_STAGE_2.md so its language matches the implemented canonical Formam baseline.
Explicitly define data/form/deliverables/ and data/form/maps/ as the canonical Stage 2 system of record.
Remove or rewrite wording that implies Formam is still only aspirational or not yet active.
Keep the language aligned with the repository's existing craft and stage vocabulary.
acceptance_criteria: The main operator-facing docs describe file-backed deliverables and goal maps as the canonical Formam storage model.
The docs no longer imply Formam is only a future design target.
A new operator can read the docs and understand how Formam records are stored and inspected.
suggested_commit_message: STAGE2-WAVEC-02: align stage 2 documentation with canonical formam storage

title: Add graceful Stage 2 closeout behavior to the ritual runner
goal: Make the ritual runner treat an exhausted Stage 2 wave as a successful closeout state by printing a final status summary and explicit Stage 2 completion message instead of failing noisily.
context: The runner now drives staged work across multiple waves and stages.
A closeout wave should end with a clean terminal state, not with a no-tasks-remaining exception or a brittle shell-path failure.
Stage 1 already benefited from graceful closeout behavior, and Stage 2 should receive the same polish.
requirements: Add a graceful no-tasks-remaining branch for the current Stage 2 wave.
Make the ritual runner emit the current task status report even when no work remains.
Make the ritual runner print an explicit completion message for Stage 2 instead of returning a non-zero exit code for the exhausted-wave case.
Keep the behavior readable and operator-friendly in the terminal.
Do not broaden this task into unrelated runner redesign.
acceptance_criteria: Running the ritual with no remaining Stage 2 Wave C tasks exits successfully.
The runner still prints a summary report when the wave backlog is exhausted.
The terminal output clearly indicates that Stage 2 is complete.
suggested_commit_message: STAGE2-WAVEC-03: add graceful stage 2 closeout behavior to the ritual runner
